title: Sandbox Session Lifecycle Management

actors.name: Runner
actors.description: Orchestrates agent execution; initiates session lifecycle via SandboxRuntimeSessionManager

actors.name: SandboxAgent
actors.description: Provides default_manifest, capabilities, run_as user identity; acquired before session use

actors.name: BaseSandboxClient
actors.description: Backend adapter; creates, resumes, and deletes sandbox sessions (e.g., UnixLocalSandboxClient, Docker)

actors.name: External Caller / Developer
actors.description: Configures SandboxRunConfig (client, session, session_state, snapshot, manifest, options)

use_cases.name: Acquire Sandbox Agent
use_cases.description: Lock a SandboxAgent for exclusive use in a run. Raises RuntimeError if agent is already active in another concurrent run.
use_cases.details: Uses threading.Lock (_SandboxConcurrencyGuard) to increment active_runs counter; also assigns a stable resume key for the agent.

use_cases.name: Ensure/Get Active Session
use_cases.description: Retrieve or create a sandbox session for an agent, starting it if needed.
use_cases.details: Returns cached _SandboxSessionResources if already created; otherwise delegates to _create_resources(). Calls resources.ensure_started().

use_cases.name: Create Fresh Sandbox Session
use_cases.description: Create a brand-new sandbox session via the configured backend client.
use_cases.details: Resolves snapshot spec (local default or NoopSnapshotSpec), effective manifest from agent.default_manifest or SandboxRunConfig.manifest, processes capabilities into manifest, calls client.create(). Configures concurrency limits on session. owns_session=True.

use_cases.name: Resume Suspended Session
use_cases.description: Restore a previously suspended session from serialized state (session_state or run_state._sandbox payload).
use_cases.details: Looks up session payload by agent resume key or agent name in sessions_by_agent dict. Deserializes via client.deserialize_session_state(). Processes manifest capabilities before resuming. owns_session=True.

use_cases.name: Inject Live Session
use_cases.description: Supply a pre-existing running session via SandboxRunConfig.session (no new session created).
use_cases.method: _create_resources() path when sandbox_config.session is not None
use_cases.details: Applies manifest diff entries to the live session if capabilities changed (entries-only; root/env/users/mounts cannot change on running sessions). owns_session=False — cleanup does NOT stop or delete the injected session.

use_cases.name: Materialize Workspace / Apply Manifest
use_cases.description: Stage files, directories, and mounts into the sandbox workspace from the Manifest.
use_cases.details: Controlled by SandboxConcurrencyLimits (manifest_entries: max concurrent entries; local_dir_files: max concurrent file copies per LocalDir entry). Capabilities can add/transform manifest entries via Capability.process_manifest().

use_cases.name: Serialize Resume State
use_cases.description: Capture the current session state into a serializable dict so the run can be resumed later.
use_cases.details: Returns None if a live injected session is used (SandboxRunConfig.session). Produces {backend_id, current_agent_key, current_agent_name, session_state, sessions_by_agent}. Each entry in sessions_by_agent: {agent_name, session_state}. State serialized via client.serialize_session_state(resources.state).

use_cases.name: Clean Up Session
use_cases.description: Gracefully stop and destroy a sandbox session after a run completes.
use_cases.details: Only executes if owns_session=True. Sequence: session.run_pre_stop_hooks() → session.stop() → session.shutdown() → client.delete(session) → session._aclose_dependencies(). Protected by asyncio.Lock to prevent double-cleanup. Errors are propagated after all steps complete.

use_cases.name: Release Acquired Agents
use_cases.description: Decrement concurrency guard on all agents after cleanup so they can be reused in future runs.
use_cases.details: Decrements _SandboxConcurrencyGuard.active_runs for each acquired agent; clears internal tracking maps.

use_cases.name: Assign Stable Resume Key
use_cases.description: Assign a deterministic, collision-free key to each agent for use in resume state serialization.
use_cases.details: Prefers stable key derived from agent graph topology (_build_agent_identity_keys_by_id), falls back to agent.name, or allocated unique key to avoid collisions.

key_classes.name: _SandboxSessionResources
key_classes.role: Internal holder for session + client + ownership flag

key_classes.name: SandboxSession
key_classes.role: Instrumented decorator around BaseSandboxSession; wraps ops with audit events and tracing spans

key_classes.name: BaseSandboxSession
key_classes.role: Abstract base; implements manifest application, workspace archive, apply_patch, PTY, concurrency semaphores

key_classes.name: SandboxSessionState
key_classes.role: Pydantic model capturing full serializable session state; base class for backend-specific subtypes

key_classes.name: SandboxRunConfig
key_classes.role: Configuration dataclass supplied by developer via RunConfig.sandbox

key_classes.name: _SandboxConcurrencyGuard
key_classes.role: Per-agent guard preventing concurrent runs; attached to SandboxAgent instance

session_creation_paths: Path 1 (Inject): SandboxRunConfig.session is set → configure materialization → diff/apply manifest entries on live session → owns_session=False
Path 2 (Resume): session_state or run_state._sandbox has payload for this agent → process manifest → client.resume(state) → owns_session=True
Path 3 (Fresh): No state available → resolve manifest + snapshot → client.create(snapshot, manifest, options) → owns_session=True

resume_state_structure.current_agent_key: string — resume key for currently-active agent
resume_state_structure.current_agent_name: string — agent name for legacy compatibility
resume_state_structure.session_state: dict — serialized SandboxSessionState for current agent

key_relationships: SandboxConcurrencyLimits is injected into BaseSandboxSession via _set_concurrency_limits()
important_notes: SandboxAgent cannot be used concurrently across multiple Runner.run() calls — enforced by threading.Lock. Resume keys are stable across runs (derived from agent graph topology). Injected live sessions (SandboxRunConfig.session) skip creation/destruction. Running injected sessions cannot change root, environment, users, groups, or mounts — only add new non-mount entries. serialize_resume_state() returns None when a live session is injected. Tracing spans emitted for sandbox.create_session, sandbox.resume_session, sandbox.cleanup_sessions, and all per-operation events.
